programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A photographic lab erroneously sends Dr Sloan someone else's photos, embroiling him in the death of an escort girl. Meanwhile, Amanda prepares to give birth. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A sinister psychiatrist offers to help Dr Sloan exact revenge on a downsizing expert who threatens the medic's job - in return for a deadly favour. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble, employed in a liquor store, is forced to become involved when a youngster holds up the place

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry is drawn into a strange case when he receives a call in the middle of the night offering him $2,000 cash and half of a $10,000 bill as a retainer

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker needs to prove that Trivette is not responsible for the death of a six-year-old boy, even though the head of internal affairs believes he is guilty. Starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man arrested for a shooting decides to represent himself in court - and proves to be far more formidable than expected. Horatio realises the only thing that can put him behind bars is the eyewitness testimony of the victim's son, but is reluctant to put the boy on the stand

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of two dishonourably discharged officers are found on board a boat belonging to Gibbs, and inquiries cast suspicion on his mentor, retired agent Mike Franks. The case proves to have wider implications - and the team finds itself battling to prevent an international confrontation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Part one of two. Forced to flee from Nevada to avoid immediate capture, Kimble's only chance to escape a roadblock is by helping a nun

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry finds a fan and some feathers and tries to track down the owner, leading him to an unusual case involving a stolen horse saddle and murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Trent and Walker fear for the life of their friend Sandoval when his cover is blown in an undercover operation to catch a drug dealer. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Henry organises a team-building exercise in the Lake District for Goddard's staff - an adventure that nearly ends in tragedy. Jacqui continues to push Stephen away, going for a scan without even telling him, but an awkward encounter with Alison during a girls' night out changes her perspective. Drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Max's Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates an extortion racket in Sun Hill's frightened immigrant community. The case brings him into contact with the family of a man he shot dead during his time with the armed response unit. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Greg puts his career at risk when he is unable to distance himself from a patient, and is forced to confront uncomfortable truths from his past. Malick struggles to stay focused on the ward as he worries about a serious complaint made against him, while Michael makes plans to spend a day off with his daughter Jasmine - only for work to get in the way once again

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. The aftermath of the explosion is seen from Charlie's perspective as he struggles to stop the body count rising even further. When the veteran nurse makes a simple mistake it is left to Connie Beauchamp to take charge and whip the overstretched team into shape. To make matters worse, the Emergency Department is then evacuated due to an unattended bag. Guest starring Holby City's Amanda Mealing

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. Stuart voices his concern that his colleague is letting personal involvement with the family cloud his judgement - an opinion borne out when Max confronts the armed youngster without telling anybody of his plans. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy puts her foot down and Terry makes Tony face reality about his mother

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian tries to get Cindy into bed while Grant and Phil take Steven and Peter to the airport. They all meet up later and get on the plane as Cindy arrives to see them depart

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia expresses her reservations about the artist Lloyd Ellis Parker to James, while Rene is distraught to learn about Sarah's pregnancy. Meanwhile, Carolyn confronts George about Regina, and Elizabeth has lunch with Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: A notorious French gangster is at large, but the only information the detectives hold is that his girlfriend has boarded a flight to Jersey. Drama, guest starring Greta Scacchi, with John Nettles and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A press conference is called when a woman mysteriously disappears, but Dangerfield suspects her boyfriend is behind it. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An activity weekend lands Dorien in hospital - where she is mortified that the private doctors might think she fraternises with the likes of Sharon and Tracey. Veteran TV weatherman Ian McCaskill appears as himself

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The Yellowcoats fight to save Fred's horses from the glue factory - with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Felix Bowness

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere decides to shoot a commercial for his supermarket empire at Grantleigh and Audrey takes advantage of the cameras to tell the world what she thinks of him. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After seeing a man skiing on a van roof, the pals are intrigued to discover it is Gavin Hinchcliffe, an old friend renowned for his eccentricity. Comedy series, guest starring Bernard Cribbins, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: The Last Detective
programme.desc: Comedy drama series from A Touch of Frost writer Richard Harris. Detective Constable 'Dangerous' Davies is an obstinate traditionalist used to being given bottom-of-the-pile jobs at his north London station until a routine inquiry offers him a lead in a 20-year-old unsolved murder case. Enlisting the help of his eccentric drinking buddy Mod Lewis, the mild-mannered detective sets out to prove himself to his colleagues. Peter Davison and Sean Hughes star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Ross has his contact book stolen by a woman he brought home from the pub, and the next morning one of his informants is found murdered, with the word 'grass' daubed in red paint at the scene of the crime. The heat is on for the inspector as he realises he has to come clean about what happened - and the pressure increases when someone else mentioned in the book is found dead. John Michie stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A millionaire businessman is found dead a year after his disappearance. Evidence suggests he was imprisoned in a cellar immediately before his death, and it's not long before a second body is discovered at the scene. The other corpse has been dead for far longer - and it seems both murders could be the work of an unlikely suspect

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam and the rest of the CID unit get to work in an effort to prove Gene's innocence when he becomes a suspect in a murder case. But as the investigation continues, further evidence is stacked up against Hunt, leading to a conclusion that rocks the very foundations of the detective's already fragile world. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Stephen gets a drenching during a moonlit walk with Jacqui and an abandoned baby opens old wounds for Ruth. Elsewhere, Vic helps Sandra in her hour of need while Peggy is away on her Italian course. Family drama, starring Sarah Lancashire and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: New consultant Serena Campbell arrives on Keller and Malick's predictions come true when she ends up clashing with Ric. Tara becomes the target of Jac's anger when she interferes in her treatment of a difficult patient, and Eddi tries to project a nonchalant air following her kiss with Luc, but her efforts to appear laid-back place her at risk of seeming unprofessional

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: New consultant Dr Adam Trueman's unethical behaviour raises a few eyebrows among the staff during his first day at work. Nathan is told his premature daughter has little chance of survival, forcing him to make a traumatic decision. Guppy contemplates turning to drugs to help him cope in the aftermath of the explosion. Tristan Gemmill joins the cast

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max, Grace and Jacob arrest a drug dealer as he makes a deal but his runner escapes, his face concealed by a hoodie. The investigation takes a serious turn when the youngster crashes the stolen car he is driving and runs off, leaving a passenger fighting for her life. The unidentified fugitive is eventually traced to a bail hostel, where three possible suspects are taken in for questioning, but a false confession makes the detectives' job doubly difficult

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian arrives home with the boys, and it's Joe and Lorraine's leaving party. Annie and Ian go to hear the election results at the Town Hall

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian loses the election but vows to stand again. Lorraine and Joe leave for Bolton. Cindy returns, determined to get her children back

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George takes Sarah to hospital after finding her at the side of the road, while Olivia continues her relationship with Lloyd, but is distraught when James discovers the affair

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: An elderly woman addicted to trashy thrillers gets a taste for life on the wrong side of the law. Dandy Nichols and Philip Davis guest star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A teenage boy arrested for possessing Ecstasy collapses in a police cell. At first it is assumed he has taken an overdose, but it subsequently emerges he is suffering from a mystery illness. Featuring an early appearance by Ashley Jensen, with Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey discovers more than she bargained for while sorting out jumble for a car-boot sale after chancing upon an old photo album. Linda Robson stars

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey resolves to sort out his marital difficulties by agreeing to a divorce. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard employs a new farm manager to improve efficiency on the estate, and Audrey is forced to learn the art of housekeeping when Brabinger falls ill. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Clegg, Billy and Truly hear an old school friend is about to die, the trio search for something - or someone - to put the spark back into his life. Comedy, guest starring Henry McGee, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Keith Clifford

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates a blackmailer at large on the London debutante scene by planting old friend Lord Robert Gospell as a mole. However, the pair fail to prevent a murder. Feature-length drama, starring Patrick Malahide and Harold Innocent

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: New information links the unexplained death of a market trader to a series of drug rapes in east London - but with that case still ongoing, Ucos officers are unable to question the chief suspect, so they have to look into their victim's past in their search for clues. Gerry finds a likely culprit in the shape of the market inspector who clamped his car, while Sandra has problems of a more personal nature when her hated mother arrives for a visit. Sheila Hancock guest stars

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Lecturer Alastair Matheson is stabbed to death in front of 30 fledgling police officers during a knife demonstration. At first it seems no more than a fatal accident in a training exercise, but when Matt, Robbie and Jackie discover that the weapon was tampered with, they find themselves faced with a murder inquiry in an all too familiar environment. Starring Alex Norton, John Michie and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The appeal of a man convicted of murder and mutilation forces Grace to question the decision she made on the case 30 years ago. Someone who appears to be copying his chilling tactics sends the team footage of a new victim having his finger cut off. At the same time, Grace receives threats and a macabre package - a jar of pickled, severed fingers. Sue Johnston and Trevor Eve star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A bereaved couple hire Lovejoy to find a pair of Regency duelling pistols and bring about the downfall of a murderer - but to complete the task he is forced to risk Jane's life and fight a duel. Anthony Valentine guest stars, with Ian McShane

Vic's proposal for a merger between the two rival rugby teams meets with opposition, while Tom Lewis has one of those days _ first he's made redundant, and then he gets into a fight with a man he suspects is having an affair with his wife. Drama, starring Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max, Grace and Jacob arrest a drug dealer as he makes a deal but his runner escapes, his face concealed by a hoodie. The unidentified fugitive is eventually traced to a bail hostel, where three possible suspects are taken in for questioning, but a false confession makes the detectives' job doubly difficult

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Holby ED comes under investigation for its handling of the bombing, with Tess, Charlie and Nathan's roles scrutinised. Matters worsen after the hospital receives unflattering headlines, and rumours circulate when it becomes clear the papers have got their information from an inside source. Guppy tries to bring Harry back from Westminster, while the team struggles to find a bed for an elderly patient

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The resident magician of a social club turns to crime to fund a gambling habit and falls prey to a blackmailer threatening to harm his girlfriend. Neil and Stevie persuade the hapless entertainer to take part in a sting operation at the club but the suspect under surveillance becomes involved in a brawl and the money vanishes into thin air

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy's return to the Square leaves Ian reeling. Bianca is sulking about Jacques. Nigel visits Julie with a raunchy French video. Peggy organises a party

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca interrupts Robbie's flirtation attempts with Mary. Grant tells Phil that he and Tiffany are doing a runner and going to a posh restaurant in town

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah is concerned about the risks involved in Rene's brain operation and refuses to give her consent, while George steps down as the Country Party candidate and attempts to face his demons regarding his strained relationship with his father. Elizabeth comes home from Sydney to attend Georgie's christening and Gino is worried about his vine saplings

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: A summer-show entertainer becomes the victim of a malicious practical joke - and Jim soon has difficulty telling truth from lies. Guest starring Rikki Fulton and Joanne Whalley

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A lung cancer patient dies suddenly, and Dangerfield discovers signs on the body suggesting euthanasia. Matters are complicated by the fact that the doctor he suspects is not only a colleague but also an old friend. Guest starring Nigel Havers

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: While reseaching her book on prisons, Dorien meets an incarcerated Chris on a visit to the Isle of Wight, and is promptly taken in by his criticisms of Sharon as a bad wife. Comedy, starring Lesley Joseph, Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin commissions a statue of himself and issues strict instructions for the unveiling - paving the way for more chaos and confusion at the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Paul Shane

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: An old school friend of Audrey's wishes to visit, but is unaware of her former classmate's unfortunate circumstances, so the former lady of the manor arranges for DeVere to entertain her. Classic comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As Barry tires of golf and looks for a new hobby, Billy's latest idea of an archery course is right on target. Long-running comedy series, starring Mike Grady, Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The death of a retired detective on his favourite golf course looks like a copycat murder. Dalziel points the finger of suspicion at local villain Danny Macer - but his relentless pursuit of his old adversary only serves to get him thrown off the case. Feature-length episode, directed by and starring Warren Clarke, with guest appearances from John Duttine, Pippa Guard and Diana Quick

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A baby is found on the doorstep of businessman Jack Bellamy, who is lying dead inside. The detectives discover the victim was a serial adulterer with a string of women who could have grounds for murder, but as the rest of the team concentrates on following the leads, Robbie finds himself growing attached to the abandoned child. Drama, starring John Michie, Blythe Duff and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team finds evidence linking a soon-to-be-released benefits fraudster with a string of murders. Unfortunately, the nature of their proof is circumstantial at best - and with only a short while to go until the end of the man's nine-month sentence, the detectives have their work cut out to solve the crimes before he is released and free to kill again. Detective drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.desc: Heledd Cynwal, Iestyn Jones and Sion Tomos Owen visit Nefyn. Heledd learns about the rich history of the area, tastes herring and raises lobster pots, Iestyn goes scuba diving to see the seagrass in Porthdinllaen, while Sion recalls the history of pilgrims and sea chanties

programme.title: Seiclo: Le Tour de France
programme.desc: Action from stage 18 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 129.7km route from Pau to Luz Ardiden

programme.title: Glannau Cymru o'r Awyr
programme.desc: A chance to enjoy and to marvel at the stunning waterfront views evident in Wales. Cameras follow the whole coastline from the air - from start to finish

programme.title: Hansh: Grid
programme.desc: A young girl questions her relationship with the hijab as she faces prejudice within society and her community while trying to live a Muslim life in the modern age

programme.title: Morning Live

Meanwhile, Jack uncovers important information in connection with the case of the missing boys, while Pratt experiences problems with a boy of his own. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to Surrey, where Izzie finds a piece of jewellery that causes quite a stir at auction, and Charlie uncovers a rare table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick tries to get through to an increasingly agitated Isaac, asking him where Ruby and Martin's children are and promising that he will not let the police hurt him. Peter is upset at the turn of events with Ash so Kathy tells him a few home truths, Chelsea lands herself in a sticky situation at Gray's house and Tommy's skills are tested at the football trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is asked to give a keynote speech at Jeni's fundraiser, but his elation is short-lived when mum Carole comes in with a burn on her arm. Is she hiding something? The cat is out of the bag when Kylie blurts out Ange's secret to Josh, while Max desperately tries to rally the troops after the disastrous findings of the Care Quality Commission

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman tries to keep up appearances as he makes a troubling discovery, while Dylan and Emma welcome an unexpected guest

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman receives a gift from Chick, but finds his date does not go according to plan. Romero's frustrations reach a tipping point

As William Parker is laid to rest, Jack has his eyes on a potential new suspect, and an attack at the cafe puts an officer's life in danger. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques and Kieran Bew

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some the most therapeutic and poignant garden transformations, including a sensory safe space for two children with autism

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A health-conscious couple from London enlist Sonali Shah to help them spend £1.3million on a detached property in Surrey with enough land to create a wellness retreat

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in rural Wiltshire to help Tom and Jen turn their old family caravan into a unique and profitable short-term rental accommodation

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to an auction in Exeter, where they come across a letter rack in an unusual shape and an Edwardian box containing a surprise

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes more people with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. First into the room is Matt with a collection of bizarre medical models. They intrigue some of the dealers and horrify others - so Matt has to work hard to seal a sale. The next item is a mid-century room divider, whose owner, Alan, is keen to sell as he has a wedding to pay for, followed by a beloved toy car. Annie thinks she will race off with a sale, but the dealers are split over the paintwork

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the craftspeople turn their attention to a pair of running shoes worn at the Berlin Olympics in 1936, a damaged photograph of a South African anti-apartheid activist, a clarinet and its case, and a prized push-along donkey

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Olivia Colman finds some surprises in her ancestral history, and her belief that most of those in her family tree emanate from Norfolk turns out to be far from the truth. Olivia is astonished to discover that she needs to travel to India to find out more about her great-great-great grandmother Harriot, who was an orphan in 1811 on a ship bound for England from Calcutta. Harriot's father was an an Englishman, but the identity of her mother, who gave birth to her in a remote Indian village, is a mystery Olivia delights in solving

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out early and how the experience changed her for the better

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Britain makes its bid to host the 2012 games, while the British team goes in search of new talent, including a nine-year-old Tom Daley beginning his sporting career. Christine Ohuruogu, the potential face of the 2012 games, discusses her dramatic fall from grace after missing three drugs tests, and her subsequent return to the track. The episode concludes with the 2008 games in Beijing, where Britain turned its fortunes around with a haul of 51 medals

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the case of bookmaker John Dickman, who, in 1910, became the last man to be hanged at Newcastle Prison. Bookman was found guilty of the murder of John Nisbet, who was robbed and shot five times

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Sam Ma has a chest pain mystery to solve when a ship's electrician is flown to Shetland for emergency treatment - but is it a heart condition or something else? Dr Lalla mentors Dr Sam Potter as she diagnoses a Lerwick man's skin complaints

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Jack gets nearer to finding the other missing boy, and the hunt for Eddie's attacker compromises Pratt's relationship with his son. Meanwhile, there is another rape, but this time the consequences are deadly, and Gina and Jack become closer following some trouble at home

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset. Jules is in his element when he meets one of the county's blacksmiths, who specialises in restoring relics of bygone eras using traditional techniques

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team head to Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been arriving from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse - not least during the current Covid-19 crisis. From its origins, the story of this beloved institution has been intertwined with one of the most divisive social and political issue of the age - immigration - and those who came to work in the NHS have found themselves fighting battles of hostility they neither sought nor expected. However, as Olusoga reveals, their stories say as much about perseverance, dedication and the overcoming of obstacles as they do about discrimination and prejudice

programme.title: Secrets of an Isis Smartphone
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar gains access to the contents of a smartphone used by three British men who left their homes to go to Syria and fight with Isis. Tthe reporter tries to understand their motivations, how they became radicalised and ultimately who is responsible for their one-way journey to certain death. Using never-before-seen videos, photos and screenshots from the device, Mobeen explores what made the trio join an estimated 900 Britons who went out to Syria and Iraq to fight with the world's most feared terrorist group

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into the case of two men sentenced to death for the murder of a Liverpool woman in 1951

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Wrexham, and discovers the story of The Ladies of Llangollen. Roo Irvine and Philip Serrell provide expert advice to today's teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost adds tall plants for shady conditions to his borders, shows how to make a window box for growing vegetables and herbs and gives tips on how to grow plants successfully in peat-free compost. Nick Bailey visits Kew Gardens to explore their collection of ferns and cameras return to the urban garden of Kate Bradbury to find out how she ensures that visiting wildlife is kept well fed throughout the summer months. Plus, the Isle of Man garden where a range of exotic plants are thriving in the microclimate on the island and a nurseryman in Kent with a passion for hydrangeas

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Leeds Rhinos v Catalans Dragons, Castleford Tigers v Salford Red Devils and Wakefield Trinity v St Helens

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Northamptonshire is the location for the antiques challenge, as Paul Martin teaches new recruits Dennie Smith and Marria Akhtar the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Bristol, Paul Martin teaches would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Roger Holbrook against Shahir Farnood. An auction showdown later reveals who has made the largest profit and made the highest percentage of profit

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing the Central regions, Pip Lacey, Ryan Simpson and Nick Deverell-Smith, go all out to impress with their main courses. Ryan hopes to elevate a ploughman's lunch into a banquet-worthy dish, while Pip pays tribute to an unusual Wimbledon icon, and Nick celebrates venison in his main course

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central chefs - Pip Lacey, Nick Deverell-Smith and Ryan Simpson - prepare their desserts, knowing this is the last chance to impress before one of them has to leave the competition. Two of them use pineapple, in reference to the fruit on top of the Wimbledon men's singles trophy, while the third celebrates the summer ice-cream van. Which two will make it through to tomorrow?

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items in Kent to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Host Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castle's secret tunnels.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer Jordan Stephens, actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty test their general knowledge

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the Diamond League meeting from Gateshead International Stadium, as the athletes involved fine-tune preparations for the Tokyo Olympics. British world champions Dina Asher-Smith and Katarina Johnson-Thompson are among those set to be involved, along with Ethiopia's Sifan Hassan who will be competing over the mile distance that she holds the world record in

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Forensic scene investigator Drew Birtles searches for evidence when a woman in her 50s alleges that an engineer working in her home indecently exposed himself. Facing a case that is difficult to prove, Drew sets to work using forensic light sources and DNA evidence to build a picture of what went on in the woman's house.

programme.title: Cunk on Britain
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk continues her historical odyssey, exploring the stories of the Tudors, the Georgians, the English Civil War, William Shakespeare, Witchfinder General Matthew Hopkins and Lord Horatio Nelson. She also looks into Henry VIII's chronic addiction to wives. With historian Kate Williams

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the final one-day international in the three-match series, which took place at Edgbaston

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: The sport's rise to prominence, beginning by examining how Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins upset the snooker establishment to help transform a game played in the backrooms of working men's clubs into a TV ratings hit. Interest in the sport had been growing thanks to the new possibilities of colour broadcasting, and in particular the weekly snooker show Pot Black, but it was the antics of the unpredictable Ulstersman that took the game stratospheric

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine the case of an 18-year-old hotel employee who was accused of murdering a titled guest in 1922

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

He also visits Plaw Hatch Farm, 200 acres of community-owned farmland that operates biodynamically according to principles first laid out in the 1920s

programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Carole Baxter are being neighbourly in Beechgrove as they swap stories over the garden fence between Brian's Alpine garden and Carole's seaside garden. Plus, a catch-up with Sophie McKilligan in her new role as gardener at Culzean Castle in Ayrshire

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Two would-be antiques dealers from East Anglia go up against each other as presenter Paul Martin shows them the tricks of the trade before they face an auction showdown

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines Australia and reveals how its formation and development has affected everything from Aboriginal history to modern-day mining, and even the evolution of its unique wildlife. He travels to the Great Australian Bight to show how the country was once joined to Antarctica, and how the koala's big, round face and fluffy ears are a result of adaptations to the change of climate after the two land masses split

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest Jordanne Whiley - who with Yui Kamiji is the current Wimbledon wheelchair doubles holder - as the programme continues its tennis-themed series

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the Northern Ireland heats sees returning contender Eddie Attwell and newcomers Joery Castel and Tommy Heaney prepare their starters for the banquet. Eddie is using a number of modern techniques, Joery hopes to impress with a tribute to 1996 Wimbledon champion Richard Krajicek, while Tommy creates a warm salad. Who will win the favour of the veteran judge?

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota at the beginning of autumn to check how abandoned bear cub Hope is progressing and is delighted to discover that her life has taken a turn for the better. However, a fresh danger is posed by the arrival of hunters in the forest and Gordon decides to walk with the animals to protect them

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team values antiques at Guildford Cathedral, where James Lewis discovers a brass figure that could be six centuries old, and Catherine Southon examines a pocket watch with a difference. Paul Martin takes time out to look at the world's first motor-racing track, and goes for a spin in a 1930s sports car

programme.title: Live T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Isa Guha presents coverage of the third and final match of the T20 series, held at The Cloudfm County Ground in Chelmsford. The teams had been due to meet in the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup early last year, but the contest was abandoned due to rain, and Heather Knight's England were eliminated by virtue of their inferior group-stage finish. This contest brings an end to the tour that previously saw the teams also face each other in one Test match, won by England, and a three-match ODI series

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Everything, Everything (2017)
programme.desc: A housebound teen with a rare immunodeficiency disease has her life turned upside down when she falls for the boy next door. Romantic drama, starring Amandla Stenberg and Nick Robinson

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of Steve Davis and the rise of promoter Barry Hearn's Matchroom group of players, which brought lucrative sponsorships into the game. By the mid-1980s, snooker was at the peak of its powers and in 1985 nearly 20 million people tuned in to see Steve Davis play Dennis Taylor in the World Championship final

programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate a lethal shooting from 1937 in which a barge captain was allegedly murdered by his best friend

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines clues hidden in the landscape to reveal how the Americas were formed from an ancient supercontinent, later named Pangaea. He finds evidence that 300 million years ago New York was at the heart of a huge mountain range, and travels to an extinct volcano in Bolivia to explore subduction - the process that has shaped South America

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland chefs prepare their seafood courses as they try to win a place at the Taste of Summer banquet honouring 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. Joery Castel combines mackerel and cucumber in his dish, Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andy Murray's apparent love of sushi and Eddie Attwell creates five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his effort

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Northern Ireland, Tommy Heaney, Joery Castel and Eddie Attwell, go all out to impress with their main courses. Barbecued dishes are on the menu for two of the chefs, with Tommy creating a surf and turf combination, while Joery aims to recreate a more American style barbecue, and Eddie opts for an unusual meat, water buffalo, for the centrepiece of his main course

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where he realises a lifelong dream by spending time with Grauer's gorillas, the largest primates on earth. He meets the fearsome alpha male Chimanuka and two babies, one a lonely orphan and as Gordon gains the trust of these mysterious, seldom seen animals, he discovers their incredible compassion and gets caught up in a battle between giant rival silverbacks for control of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty, Jordan Stephens, Sally Lindsay and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A tiger undergoes surgery, but the vets are soon faced with a difficult decision. A cockatoo ruffles some feathers, and the park's white rhinos enjoy a detoxing charcoal spa

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Monty travels to Japan in spring to explore different aspects of the nation's gardens. Amidst the cherry blossoms, he begins his journey by visiting the earliest surviving boating garden of the Heian period. He also looks at the rolling green moss of a Buddhist garden and learns the secrets of creating a Zen landscape before visiting an unconventional garden created by a modern design legend. The presenter also learns about the art of Japanese stonemasonry, the famous tea ceremonies and receives a lesson in the delicate art of traditional flower arranging

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Royal St. George's Golf Club in Sandwich, Kent. The tournament was due to be played at this venue last year for the first time since Darren Clarke prevailed in 2011. However, it was cancelled due to the Covid-19 pandemic, meaning Shane Lowry enters as the defending champion, having taken the honours at Portrush in 2019

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show, featuring a different topic each week. Here, comedians Jason Byrne, Marlon Davis, Micky Flanagan, Jessica Fostekew, Guz Khan, Sean Lock and Sindhu Vee perform routines about children

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Forensic scene investigator Drew Birtles searches for evidence when a woman in her 50s alleges that an engineer working in her home indecently exposed himself. Facing a case that is difficult to prove, Drew sets to work using forensic light sources and DNA evidence to build a picture of what went on in the woman's house.

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: Desperate for success and tempted by the lucrative rewards brought by towing the line, Jimmy White joined Barry Hearn in the hope he could be turned into a champion like his new stablemate Steve Davis. With White as the hot-favourite and with the crowd urging him on against 21-year-old Stephen Hendry, the final of 1990 world championship turned out to be a pivotal moment in British snooker and paved the way for what was to come in the next decade

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Ben and Myles visit an abandoned hotel in search of money owed to a former employee, and the Sheriffs come up against a car dealer who is refusing to refund a customer

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a mountain against the backdrop of a clear, blue sky

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. The Las Vegas-style shows and exotic locations often took second billing to the drama and heartbreak behind the scenes

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Sir Humphrey tries to conceal information due for release under the 30-year rule. Jim Hacker investigates, and soon uncovers a priceless weapon in his battle with the civil service. Comedy, starring Paul Eddington, with Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: The Goods begin to reap their just rewards for the hard work they have put into the garden, although Barbara is too tired and bored to appreciate the benefits. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: The documentary about the American writer reveals how, having achieved a level of fame rarely seen in the literary world, Hemingway settled in Key West with Pauline Pfeiffer. He now spent his time seeking out new conquests in the natural world - marlin fishing in the Gulf Stream and hunting in Africa. With readings by Meryl Streep, Patricia Clarkson, Keri Russell, Joe Morton and Josh Lucas

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The journey progresses to Key West to witness the 18th Annual Papa Hemingway lookalike contest, then to Uganda, where the novelist survived two air crashes in just 48 hours. Michael then journeys up the Nile to witness Murchison Falls and makes his way to Venice for the carnival

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Larry Krasner and his team carefully evaluate cases, recommending new and sometimes more lenient sentences to give those imprisoned for committing serious crimes a second chance

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Larry Krasner supports legal injection clinics to clean up the streets in the poorest parts of Philadelphia, only for the local cops to blame him for a spike in crime

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson, reveal that eugenics - the controversial idea that was a driving force behind the Nazi death camps - originated in the upper echelons of the British scientific community. They also examine its legacy and continued influence today

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Gramat tries a desperate gambit to regain the initiative on Mars. Sci-fi drama, starring Clément Aubert. In French

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. The Las Vegas-style shows and exotic locations often took second billing to the drama and heartbreak behind the scenes

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: The documentary about the American writer reveals how, having achieved a level of fame rarely seen in the literary world, Hemingway settled in Key West with Pauline Pfeiffer. He now spent his time seeking out new conquests in the natural world - marlin fishing in the Gulf Stream and hunting in Africa. With readings by Meryl Streep, Patricia Clarkson, Keri Russell, Joe Morton and Josh Lucas

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a west coast seascape, with turbulent waves, jagged cliffs and dark night skies

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. The Las Vegas-style shows and exotic locations often took second billing to the drama and heartbreak behind the scenes

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: In the south-west of Japan, the Ryukyu Islands stretch towards the tropics and all life on them is influenced by the power of the sea, with volcanos and typhoons forces to be reckoned with. Michelle Dockery narrates a journey beginning at an island at the top of the chain and travelling south, revealing unexpected stories of isolation, unique wildlife and unsolved mysteries

programme.title: Tails You Win: The Science of Chance
programme.desc: Professor David Spiegelhalter tries to get to grips with chance and how it works. He tackles such questions as whether people are wise to take an umbrella when they go out, to ways of maximising the chances of living to 100, as he tries to establish if luck and fate are embedded in the fabric of the universe. Along the way, he discovers hidden patterns and shapes in a book written exclusively with numbers and explains why going sky-diving only marginally increases his risk of dying sooner rather than later

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The downfall of the former CEO of Renault-Nissan, who went from industry leader to international fugitive after being charged with financial crimes, prompting him to escape from Japan to Lebanon

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott discover how the search for extra-terrestrial life in the universe has been reported by the BBC over the past six decades, from the birth of radio astronomy in the 1960s to the discovery of the first planets outside of our own solar system in the 1990s, and right up to probes exploring our neighbouring planets in the present day

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Jeanne is overwhelmed by the memories of her father, while the Ulysses crew come under threat from the impatient Z-2 military men. In French, Russian, English and German

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: The mums and dad join the merry-go-round of secondary school open days as they face the choice of where to send their children, with Julia panicking about which catchment area she lives in and even considering a fake religious conversion to beat the system. As Kevin's divorce progresses, Liz helps him navigate the shark-infested world of lawyers, and makes a worrying discovery about her own situation along the way, while Amanda struggles with playing second fiddle to Meg's illness

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. The Las Vegas-style shows and exotic locations often took second billing to the drama and heartbreak behind the scenes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Return to The Cruise

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: The Importance of Being Oscar
programme.desc: A star-studded documentary-drama providing an account of Oscar Wilde's glittering career before his trial for homosexual crimes and his tragic fall from grace. Wilde enthusiasts and experts such Stephen Fry and Wilde's grandson Merlin Holland provide revelatory accounts of how the writer's own life informed his work, intercut with adaptations of his comedies and books performed by a cast including Freddie Fox, Claire Skinner, Anna Chancellor and James Fleet

programme.title: Wilde (1997)
programme.desc: Biopic of author and acclaimed wit Oscar Wilde, focusing on his affair with Lord Alfred Douglas at a time when homosexual liaisons were illegal. Refusing to flee the country when their relationship was uncovered, he was subsequently jailed, enduring two years of hard labour. Starring Stephen Fry, Jude Law, Vanessa Redgrave and Jennifer Ehle

programme.title: Missions

programme.desc: Happy discovers his wobbly tooth has come out and he has a problem remembering where he was when it fell out

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: On a car trip to the dump, Bluey discovers that Dad is throwing out her old drawings.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch turns the canals of Venice into roads

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter decides to set Mr Tod and Tommy Brack against each other - but learns that breaking the food chain is harder than expected

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Tweak must help her friend Sandy the sea turtle escape a hungry tiger shark

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: What if there was a way to explore every possible question? Imagine that! Learn the power of asking questions with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: At the end of a busy day, E just wants to sing himself to sleep - but Little Bo Peep and friends have other plans

programme.title: Kit and Pup
programme.desc: Kit and Pup head to the park, where they learn about liquid and solid things

programme.title: Maddie, the Zoo and You
programme.desc: Maddie learns about communication and finds out how we use our senses.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is trying to do some gardening, but Tee wants to hunt for treasure!

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say worm and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We explore everything about worms. There is a long worm, a little worm and a squirmy worm

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug go to Buckingham Palace where they meet Queen Victoria.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos visit a beach and watch a boy build sandcastles. He makes more and more sandcastles, and the Twirlywoos decide they want to help

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin has lots of Autumn fun with his friends. While Mr Tumble is struggling to play football because lots of leaves are blocking the goal

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go for a walk on a lovely sunny day, but then clouds appear and it starts to rain, but then a rainbow appears

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are rushing around, so Duggee shows them how to enjoy things at a slower pace. However, they come across Tortoise who needs to get to the hospital as his wife is having babies, so Duggee and the squirrels need to get him there fast

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wingston unleashes a computer bug on the monsters' computers, because he's not listening.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Shuffle Snuffler sniffs its way through the jungle. It is horrified when it smells B.O.T.! Can B.O.T. avoid being sneezed to pieces and complete this mission?

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day knows young animals make great explorers. In Antarctica a seal pup takes its first journey under the ice. Leopard cubs must climb as they explore the riverbank with mum. A tiny goby fish faces a huge obstacle - getting up a waterfall

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to a bakery to buy some bread. On the way they use magic to escape from a gate, discover a pointy tower, run around the swirly rock and pretend to be diggers!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: You Are Old, Father William by Lewis Carroll Wynken, Blynken and Nod by Eugene Field Look at what my hands can do! In this two poem episode, Aimee and Ashley are visited by a silvery, slithery eel and lots of twinkling herring fish. Ashley meets Bobbles who takes the role of old Father William to do some impressive back-flips and balance an eel on the end of his nose. Aimee also sails the skies in a wooden shoe with Leapy the Lion, Dixie the Dog and Click Clack the Crab as magical men who are fishing for stars

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a costume tower and a magic train, and watch as Book Girl makes a drum kit.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: It is Sid's turn for an industrious adventure as he steps through the magic curtains and becomes a carpenter

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: It's Snoof Air Show day and the excited Olobobs try flying a kite. When it gets stuck in a tree, they make Loopy Longtail reach up to the kite - though he then takes off with it

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The team goes where no Go Jetter has gone before, when Glitch tries to launch into space by attaching huge rocket boosters to Grim HQ

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back in time three million years to the Great Rift Valley in Africa to meet one of man's very own ancient ancestors, the australopithecus. During his adventure he has a narrow escape from an angry relative of the elephant and takes a trip in an inflatable dinghy over a waterfall

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: George gets locked inside Mrs Hobbs' house and Waffle has a chance to prove himself to Mrs Hobbs. Can he rescue George?

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Flora loses her flute, so Jack and Salty help her find it

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: The spider helps Tig overcome her fear of public toilets when they go to a painting day

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say boat and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different boats; a big boat, a small boat, and a boat you stick on the wall

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock I has a dance party by herself

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: What if there was a way to explore every possible question? Imagine that! Learn the power of asking questions with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: The monster and his friends go on a rollercoaster adventure and figure out why Stan Tall is in such a spin

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour silver, like silver-looking zips, and explore objects that appear silver-coloured in towns and cities. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour silver.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Using a sign language called Makaton, Something Special helps its audience learn how to communicate as well as develop their language in a fun and exciting way. Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent! starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It is night time in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies are all sleeping, apart from Tinky Winky. Stepping outside, Tinky Winky sees a star in the sky which sings Twinkle Twinkle. The other Teletubbies are woken so, they can enjoy the special twinkly star. In Tummy Tales, a girl looks up to the night sky to spot the twinkliest star.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters join Ubercorn in the Go Jet Academy to try out a brand new super vacuum that reportedly can suck up bad weather

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The friends put together scrapbooks of all their favourite childhood memories

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory and Ferne meet some feathered friends and the vets that care for them

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It's time for the annual Animal Obstacle Course Race - the animals need to work together in teams to complete the course. Duggee has his Obstacle Course badge, together with the squirrels they form the Red team and the fun begins

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter proves that he has got what it takes to be part of a squirrel tribe when he competes for his own treetop hideout

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: On a car trip to the dump, Bluey discovers that Dad is throwing out her old drawings.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grim HQ's heating gets stuck on, Glitch looks for a cold place to land and cool down. Meanwhile, the Go Jetters enjoy the cool sights of Harbin Ice City in China. Every year sculptors at the festival create realistic models of world famous landmarks from blocks of ice and Lars is attempting to carve the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the monster finger puppet and remembers the day she got it. When Mack gets a delivery of new toys, they remind him of the fun he and Molly used to have with finger puppets when they were younger. Alice has always wondered what it would be like to have a sibling. Mack thinks back and realises that things don’t always go to plan when Molly’s around, but then, he sometimes has a habit of messing things up too. He tells Alice he knows he can always rely on Molly to help him out, even if it means he has to share Daisy with her too. Alice thinks Molly and Mack have a special relationship. Molly arrives and agrees. She couldn’t ask for a better big brother!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Mrs Hobbs interviews for a cat sitter for George, and Waffle wants the job.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go to the hairdresser's to see how Gran Gran gets her hair cut.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The pair discover a Lemon Cafe in the park, but it seems to be nearly closing time

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Moon Baby, Pepi Nana and all the friends find a toy roundabout has been left in the Toy House. Each chooses a vehicle to ride in and they have a race. Mr Onion thinks his very slow Bumparoller will be last, but he's in for a surprise

programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: Holby City
